comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. comment.body:
Not reproduced on my end. At this time, please do the following. I'll ask my colleagues to check this. @AdguardTeam/filters-maintainers please also take a look at this.

---
Try to update the filters (wait almost one minute there), clear browser data, restart it, open the site.
If that doesn't work, you should re-enable the content blockers in Safari settings (wait about the same time as when you update the filters).

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -3963,6 +3960,9 @@ m.tokopedia.com#$#div[data-testid="divOverlay"] { display: none!important; }
 ! causes some issues: https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardForMac/issues/550
 ~odaibako.net,~lineup.withgoogle.com,~vert.sh,~search.brave.com,~status.im,~lafenice.io,~kurer-sreda.ru,~chorki.com,~zattoo.com,~zaycev.net,~hitta.se,~mail.ru,~facebook.com,~instagram.com,~drive.google.com$$link[rel="manifest"]
 !
+x.com###react-root div[data-testid="BottomBar"]:has(> div[class^="css-"] div[class^="css-"][style^="flex-grow: 1"]:has(> a[href^="/login"]) + div[class^="css-"][style^="flex-grow: 1; margin-left:"]:has(> a[href^="/i/flow/signup"]))
comment.body:
Got it. However, I think it's better to rewrite it to something like this or even make a more specific rule with one `:has()`. I see the flickering element with your rule.
```adblock
x.com###react-root div[data-testid="BottomBar"]:has(> div[class^="css-"] div[class^="css-"][style^="flex-grow: 1; margin-left:"] > a[href^="/i/flow/signup"])
```

issue.title: dn.ru
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
 [https://dn.ru](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdn.ru)
### Comment
> Good afternoon. After enabling Adguard, DNS lookups stopped working on many websites. This happened because diginetica.net is blacklisted by Adguard, even though over a thousand websites in Russia use diginetica's services for intelligent search. Here's a short list of websites where searches don't work with Adguard enabled:
> https://auchan.ru,
> https://kotofoto.ru,
> https://dn.ru,
> https://amwine.ru,
> https://alltime.ru,
> and this is just a small list. Please whitelist diginetica.net, as this service works on most websites in Russia. This is not an ad or a third-party tracker. Thank you in advance.
```
Username: @bazhenovdev
```

### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 </summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://reports.adguard.info/stcdn/sitereports/dvqb0387vbsc840gwowckcwcws4skgos1frbyw5c1w.png?nc=1)
</details>
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 2 </summary>

  ![Screenshot 2](https://reports.adguard.info/stcdn/sitereports/4whoiq1s15s08gc0w0sgw4c8ckwck8o2cu86zeg4f4.png?nc=1)
</details>
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 3 </summary>

  ![Screenshot 3](https://reports.adguard.info/stcdn/sitereports/17jcequdogqo4kgokwsc0w40sscc4go9wqxzw1osm8.png?nc=1)

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
